comics.title: Time Machine Conversation
comics.alt: It's possible to do sea navigation without a compass, but you'll have to get some spoilers from the Polynesians.

comics.title: Flag Design
comics.alt: Every place has a local cryptid; more places need a local Pictish Beast, a creature in historical art that's drawn so weirdly that no one can tell what animal it's supposed to be.

comics.title: Neutrino Project
comics.alt: We definitely put the pool in a mine for shielding. It was absolutely not to hide it from the funding people.

comics.title: 182.8 Meters
comics.alt: They rounded down to 182.8 instead of rounding up to 182.9 because 182.9 might make the statement incorrect.

comics.title: Particle Census
comics.alt: Remember, your answers to the physics census are confidential; we will not be issuing Pauli exclusion principle citations.

comics.title: Speedrun
comics.alt: Usain Bolt holds the world record in the 100 meter speedrun.

comics.title: Results Age
comics.alt: Please, we need your help. Our research suggests you're the last living descendant of the person who knew how to format this config file.

comics.title: Pullback Drive
comics.alt: "How does the spring not run out almost immediately?" "We pull it back REALLY far."

comics.title: Crystal Gazing
comics.alt: 'Beyond that lies a vale of fire through which my vision cannot penetrate' is the kind of fun thing geologists, heliophysicists, and early universe cosmologists have a lot of opportunities to say.

comics.title: Aperiodic Table
comics.alt: Scientists occasionally invent alternative periodic table layouts, which is usually a sign that they don't have enough enrichment in their enclosures.

comics.title: Horizontal Stabilizers
comics.alt: It started as a mistake that everyone was afraid to admit to, and then it stuck because removing it 'looks silly.'.

comics.title: Bottle
comics.alt: "I know it seems impossible, but the trick is that I sailed in here when I was very young."

comics.title: Simple Machines
comics.alt: It's hard to decide which simple machine system to invest in. DeWalt makes a great lever and inclined plane, but I hear Milwaukee's wheel-and-axles are really good.

comics.title: Soniferous Aether
comics.alt: Imagine you could ride alongside a sound wave. It would probably be pretty cool, right? We're putting in a departmental budget request to buy a really fast plane so we can check it out.

comics.title: Husband and Wife
comics.alt: Borat came out twenty years ago this year--closer to the breakup of the Soviet Union than to today--but it honestly feels like it's been even longer, somehow.

comics.title: Border Message
comics.alt: Thanks to differences in logging regulations, the messages actually turned out to be visible from the air.

comics.title: Types of Board Game
comics.alt: I can't believe Candles of Vienna caved to commercial pressure and added the Goku expansion.

comics.title: Europa Missions
comics.alt: Before resurfacing, they promise to inspect the ice for any evidence of hockey-playing life.

comics.title: Make It Myself
comics.alt: It's not as big a loss as it looks, because now I have leftover supplies, which will help me talk myself into doing this all over again with a new project!

comics.title: Countdown Standard
comics.alt: Anyone who is caught counting 'three ... two ... one ... zero ... GO!' will be punished with a lifetime of eating only ISO standard food samples.
